---
title: "final_latex"
output: pdf_document
date: "2024-12-04"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

\subsection*{1. Likelihood}
The likelihood function for the binary outcome \(y_n \in \{0, 1\}\) is:
\[
y_n \sim \text{Bernoulli}(\theta_n),
\]
where
\[
\theta_n = \text{logit}^{-1}(\alpha_{\text{qualification}[n]} + \mathbf{x}_n \cdot \boldsymbol{\beta}),
\]
and \(\text{logit}^{-1}(z) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-z}}\).

\subsection*{2. Priors}
The priors for the model parameters are:
\[
\alpha_j \sim \text{Normal}(\mu_\alpha, \sigma_\alpha), \quad j = 1, \dots, J,
\]
\[
\beta_k \sim \text{Normal}(0, 3), \quad k = 1, \dots, K.
\]

\subsection*{3. Hyperpriors}
The hyperpriors for the hierarchical parameters are:
\[
\mu_\alpha \sim \text{Normal}(0, 3),
\]
\[
\sigma_\alpha \sim \text{Cauchy}(0, 2.5).
\]

\subsection*{4. Posterior Distribution}
The posterior distribution for the parameters is proportional to:
\[
p(\boldsymbol{\alpha}, \boldsymbol{\beta}, \mu_\alpha, \sigma_\alpha \mid \mathbf{y}) \propto \prod_{n=1}^N \text{Bernoulli}(y_n \mid \theta_n) 
\cdot \prod_{j=1}^J \text{Normal}(\alpha_j \mid \mu_\alpha, \sigma_\alpha)
\cdot \prod_{k=1}^K \text{Normal}(\beta_k \mid 0, 3)
\cdot \text{Normal}(\mu_\alpha \mid 0, 3)
\cdot \text{Cauchy}(\sigma_\alpha \mid 0, 2.5).
\]

\subsection*{5. Log-Likelihood}
The log-likelihood for each observation is:
\[
\text{log-likelihood}_n = y_n \cdot \log(\theta_n) + (1 - y_n) \cdot \log(1 - \theta_n),
\]
where \(\theta_n = \text{logit}^{-1}(\alpha_{\text{qualification}[n]} + \mathbf{x}_n \cdot \boldsymbol{\beta})\).

\end{document}
